full_name: UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, Sixth Revised Edition, Sub-Section 38.3: Lithium metal and lithium ion batteries
publication_date: Sixth Revised Edition (2015), with February 2016 Corrigendum

conditions.sample_size: 10 cells (undischarged/fully charged), 4 small batteries, 4 large batteries (per 38.3.3)

criteria.description: No leakage, no venting, no disassembly, no rupture and no fire. Open circuit voltage after testing shall not be less than 90% of voltage prior to procedure.
criteria.voltage_exemption: Voltage requirement not applicable to cells/batteries at fully discharged states (38.3.4.1.3)

tests.original_text_snippet: Section 38.3.4.1.2: Test cells and batteries shall be stored at a pressure of 11.6 kPa or less for at least six hours at ambient temperature (20 ± 5°C). Section 38.3.4.1.3: Cells and batteries meet this requirement if there is no leakage, no venting, no disassembly, no rupture and no fire and if the open circuit voltage of each test cell or battery after testing is not less than 90% of its voltage immediately prior to this procedure.

conditions.high_temp: 72 ± 2°C for at least 6 hours (12 hours for large cells/batteries)
conditions.low_temp: -40 ± 2°C for at least 6 hours (12 hours for large cells/batteries)
conditions.sample_size: 10 cells (undischarged/fully charged), 4 small batteries, 4 large batteries (per 38.3.3)

criteria.description: No leakage, no venting, no disassembly, no rupture and no fire. Open circuit voltage after testing shall not be less than 90% of voltage prior to procedure.
criteria.voltage_exemption: Voltage requirement not applicable to cells/batteries at fully discharged states (38.3.4.2.3)

tests.original_text_snippet: Section 38.3.4.2.2: Test cells and batteries are to be stored for at least six hours at a test temperature equal to 72 ± 2 °C, followed by storage for at least six hours at a test temperature equal to - 40 ± 2 °C. The maximum time interval between test temperature extremes is 30 minutes. This procedure is to be repeated until 10 total cycles are complete. Section 38.3.4.2.3: Cells and batteries meet this requirement if there is no leakage, no venting, no disassembly, no rupture and no fire and if the open circuit voltage of each test cell or battery after testing is not less than 90% of its voltage immediately prior to this procedure.

conditions.frequency_range: 7 Hz to 200 Hz and back to 7 Hz
conditions.cycle_count: 12 cycles (total 3 hours) for each of 3 mutually perpendicular mounting positions
conditions.orientation: One direction must be perpendicular to terminal face
conditions.small_batteries_acceleration: 1 gn to 18 Hz, then 0.8 mm amplitude (1.6 mm total excursion) to 50 Hz (8 gn), then 8 gn to 200 Hz
conditions.large_batteries_acceleration: 1 gn to 18 Hz, then 0.8 mm amplitude to 25 Hz (2 gn), then 2 gn to 200 Hz

criteria.description: No leakage, no venting, no disassembly, no rupture and no fire during and after test. Open circuit voltage after testing in third perpendicular mounting position shall not be less than 90% of voltage prior to procedure.
criteria.voltage_exemption: Voltage requirement not applicable to cells/batteries at fully discharged states (38.3.4.3.3)

tests.original_text_snippet: Section 38.3.4.3.2: The vibration shall be a sinusoidal waveform with a logarithmic sweep between 7 Hz and 200 Hz and back to 7 Hz traversed in 15 minutes. This cycle shall be repeated 12 times for a total of 3 hours for each of three mutually perpendicular mounting positions of the cell. Section 38.3.4.3.3: Cells and batteries meet this requirement if there is no leakage, no venting, no disassembly, no rupture and no fire during the test and after the test and if the open circuit voltage of each test cell or battery directly after testing in its third perpendicular mounting position is not less than 90% of its voltage immediately prior to this procedure.

conditions.cell_acceleration: 150 gn peak acceleration, 6 ms pulse duration (large cells: 50 gn, 11 ms alternative)
conditions.small_battery_acceleration: Minimum 50 gn or result of formula: Acceleration = sqrt(30000/mass), whichever is smaller, 6 ms pulse
conditions.large_battery_acceleration: Minimum based on formula: Acceleration = sqrt(30000/mass), whichever is smaller, 11 ms pulse
conditions.shock_count: 3 shocks positive + 3 shocks negative in each of 3 mutually perpendicular positions (total 18 shocks)

criteria.description: No leakage, no venting, no disassembly, no rupture and no fire. Open circuit voltage after testing shall not be less than 90% of voltage prior to procedure.
criteria.voltage_exemption: Voltage requirement not applicable to cells/batteries at fully discharged states (38.3.4.4.3)

tests.original_text_snippet: Section 38.3.4.4.2: Each cell shall be subjected to a half-sine shock of peak acceleration of 150 gn and pulse duration of 6 milliseconds. Each cell or battery shall be subjected to three shocks in the positive direction and to three shocks in the negative direction in each of three mutually perpendicular mounting positions for a total of 18 shocks. Section 38.3.4.4.3: Cells and batteries meet this requirement if there is no leakage, no venting, no disassembly, no rupture and no fire and if the open circuit voltage of each test cell or battery after testing is not less than 90% of its voltage immediately prior to this procedure.

conditions.preconditioning: Heated to homogeneous stabilized temperature of 57 ± 4°C (minimum 6 hours for small cells/batteries, 12 hours for large)
conditions.resistance: Total external resistance less than 0.1 ohm
conditions.duration: At least 1 hour after case temperature returns to 57 ± 4°C, or for large batteries, decreased by half of maximum temperature increase and remains below that value
conditions.ambient_temp: Short circuit and cooling conducted at least at ambient temperature

criteria.description: External temperature shall not exceed 170°C. No disassembly, no rupture and no fire during test and within 6 hours after test.

tests.original_text_snippet: Section 38.3.4.5.2: The cell or battery at 57 ± 4 °C shall be subjected to one short circuit condition with a total external resistance of less than 0.1 ohm. This short circuit condition is continued for at least one hour after the cell or battery external case temperature has returned to 57 ± 4 °C. Section 38.3.4.5.3: Cells and batteries meet this requirement if their external temperature does not exceed 170 °C and there is no disassembly, no rupture and no fire during the test and within six hours after the test.

tests.applicability: Impact: cylindrical cells ≥18.0 mm diameter. Crush: prismatic, pouch, coin/button, cylindrical <18.0 mm

conditions_impact.apparatus: 15.8 ± 0.1 mm diameter Type 316 stainless steel bar (at least 6 cm long)
conditions_impact.orientation: Longitudinal axis parallel to flat surface, perpendicular to bar
conditions_impact.sample_requirement: Test cells/component cells not previously subjected to other tests

conditions_crush.speed: Approximately 1.5 cm/s at first point of contact
termination_criteria: Voltage drops by at least 100 mV
Cell deformed by 50% or more of original thickness
conditions_crush.orientation_cylindrical: Force applied perpendicular to longitudinal axis

criteria.description: External temperature shall not exceed 170°C. No disassembly and no fire during test and within 6 hours after test.

tests.original_text_snippet: Section 38.3.4.6.2: A 9.1 kg ± 0.1 kg mass is to be dropped from a height of 61 ± 2.5 cm at the intersection of the bar and sample. Each sample is to be subjected to only a single impact. Section 38.3.4.6.3: A cell or component cell is to be crushed between two flat surfaces. The crushing is to be continued until the first of three options is reached: (a) 13 kN ± 0.78 kN, (b) voltage drops by 100 mV, or (c) cell deformed by 50% or more. Section 38.3.4.6.4: Cells meet this requirement if external temperature does not exceed 170°C and there is no disassembly and no fire during test and within 6 hours after test.

tests.applicability: Rechargeable batteries or single cell rechargeable batteries with overcharge protection

conditions.charge_current: Twice the manufacturer's recommended maximum continuous charge current
conditions.min_voltage_low: For recommended charge voltage ≤18V: lesser of (2 × max charge voltage) or 22V
conditions.min_voltage_high: For recommended charge voltage >18V: 1.2 × max charge voltage
conditions.sample_size: 4 small batteries (first cycle, fully charged), 4 small batteries (after 50 cycles), 2 large batteries (first cycle), 2 large batteries (after 25 cycles) - per 38.3.3(d)

criteria.description: No disassembly and no fire during test and within 7 days after test.

exemptions: Batteries not equipped with overcharge protection that are designed for use only as component in another battery or equipment that affords such protection (38.3.3(d))
Component cells that are not transported separately from the battery need only be tested to T.6 and T.8 (38.3.2.1)
tests.original_text_snippet: Section 38.3.4.7.2: The charge current shall be twice the manufacturer's recommended maximum continuous charge current. The duration of the test shall be 24 hours. Section 38.3.4.7.3: Rechargeable batteries meet this requirement if there is no disassembly and no fire during the test and within seven days after the test.

conditions.power_supply: 12 V D.C. power supply in series
conditions.initial_current: Equal to maximum discharge current specified by manufacturer
conditions.apparatus: Resistive load of appropriate size and rating in series with test cell
conditions.duration: Time interval (hours) = Rated capacity (Ah) / Initial test current (A)
conditions.sample_size: 10 primary cells (fully discharged), 10 primary component cells, 10 rechargeable cells (first cycle, fully discharged), 10 rechargeable cells (after 50 cycles) - per 38.3.3(e)

criteria.description: No disassembly and no fire during test and within 7 days after test.

exemptions: Component cells that are not transported separately from the battery need only be tested to T.6 and T.8 (38.3.2.1)
tests.original_text_snippet: Section 38.3.4.8.2: Each cell shall be forced discharged at ambient temperature by connecting it in series with a 12 V D.C. power supply at an initial current equal to the maximum discharge current specified by the manufacturer. Each cell shall be forced discharged for a time interval (in hours) equal to its rated capacity divided by the initial test current (in ampere). Section 38.3.4.8.3: Primary or rechargeable cells meet this requirement if there is no disassembly and no fire during the test and within seven days after the test.

exemptions_summary.exemption: Component cells not transported separately from battery need only be tested to T.6 and T.8 (38.3.2.1)

Rechargeable single cell batteries with overcharge protection shall be subjected to T.7; those without protection designed for use in equipment that affords protection are exempt (38.3.2.1, 38.3.3(d))
Test cells/component cells must not have been previously subjected to other tests (38.3.4.6.3)
Batteries not equipped with overcharge protection designed for use as component in another battery/equipment that affords protection (38.3.3(d))
Battery assemblies with aggregate lithium content ≤500g (or Li-ion ≤6200 Wh) assembled from tested batteries need only be tested to T.3, T.4, T.5 (and T.7 if rechargeable) (38.3.3(f))
Large battery assemblies (>500g lithium or >6200 Wh) do not need testing if verified as preventing overcharge, short circuits, and over discharge between batteries (38.3.3(g))
Voltage retention requirement (90%) not applicable to cells/batteries at fully discharged states (38.3.4.1.3, 38.3.4.2.3, 38.3.4.3.3, 38.3.4.4.3)

test_sequence_requirements.T.1_to_T.5: Shall be conducted in sequence on the same cell or battery (38.3.4)
test_sequence_requirements.T.6_and_T.8: Shall be conducted using not otherwise tested cells or batteries (38.3.4)
test_sequence_requirements.T.7: May be conducted using undamaged batteries previously used in T.1 to T.5 for cycled battery testing (38.3.4)

metadata.extraction_method: Manual reading and analysis of complete .md file
metadata.notes: UN38.3 is a transport testing standard. Tests T.1-T.5 are sequential on same sample; T.6 and T.8 require fresh samples; T.7 may use cycled samples from T.1-T.5. Multiple exemptions apply based on battery type, configuration, and intended use.
